en.extract:
Sabine's spinetail ( SAY-bin; Rhaphidura sabini, sometimes spelled Raphidura sabini) is a species of swift in the family Apodidae.
It is widespread across the African tropical rainforest.
Its common name and Latin binomial commemorate General Sir Edward Sabine.


Diet
The Sabine's spinetail is a frugivore.

'''Sabine's spinetail''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|s|eɪ|b|ɪ|n}} {{respell|SAY|bin}}; '''''Rhaphidura sabini''''', sometimes spelled '''''Raphidura sabini''''') is a species of [[Swift (bird)|swift]] in the family [[Apodidae]].
It is widespread across the [[Tropical rainforest|African tropical rainforest]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite thesis |title=The Genetic Effects of Forest Fragmentation on Amazonian Forest Birds. |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.31390/gradschool_disstheses.5612 |publisher=Louisiana State University Libraries |first=John |last=Bates}}</ref>

Its common name and Latin binomial commemorate General Sir [[Edward Sabine]].

== Diet ==
The Sabine's spinetail is a frugivore.<ref name=":0" />
